title: Cinnamon Fruit Oatmeal
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no animal-derived ingredients that contribute cholesterol. The oatmeal, chia, flax, almond milk, cinnamon, fruit, and honey contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the bowl is built around oats, chia seeds, flax seeds, and fruit, making it fiber-forward and supportive of LDL lowering. Flavor and texture come from cinnamon, fruit, and light sweetness rather than saturated fat.
intro: This is a no-fuss breakfast bowl built for real life, not for show. Instant oatmeal gets extra staying power from chia and flax, frozen fruit softens right into the hot oats, and a little cinnamon and honey pull it together into something simple, warm, and easy to keep in rotation.

stepGroups.title: Build the Bowl
items: In a microwave-safe bowl, combine the instant oatmeal, chia seeds, ground flax seeds, unsweetened almond milk, and cinnamon. Stir well.

stepGroups.title: Heat with the Fruit
items: Spread the frozen fruit over the top of the oatmeal. Microwave until the oatmeal is hot and thickened and the fruit is warmed through, about 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 minutes depending on the microwave and the fruit used.

stepGroups.title: Finish and Serve
items: Stir the fruit into the hot oatmeal, breaking up any larger pieces as needed. Let the bowl stand for 1 to 2 minutes so the chia and flax fully hydrate and the texture settles. Drizzle with the honey and serve.
